panels.caption: 2 AM. The machine never sleeps.
panels.prompt: Classic newspaper comic strip art style. Black and white with very limited spot color — like Calvin and Hobbes Sunday strip or Peanuts. Clean confident ink lines, expressive characters, strong silhouettes. A figure at a computer desk late at night — the scene simple and clear, the drawing economical. Hand-lettered feeling, classic comic strip composition. Warm spot color on the glowing screen only. Generous white space. No speech bubbles.

panels.caption: Something's wrong.
panels.prompt: Newspaper comic strip style, B&W with spot color. Close-up of a face with a classic cartoon expression of dawning realization — eyebrows raised, slight frown, the pen-tapping-lip of someone seeing a problem. Clean simple lines. Expressive, economical, humorous undertone even in the serious moment. Strong ink lines, white background, minimal detail. The face is clear and readable from across a room. No text.

panels.caption: The logs don't lie. The logic does.
panels.prompt: Newspaper comic strip art, B&W with one accent color. The error on screen shown with a classic cartoon visual indicator — a small explosion symbol, a red X, something that reads immediately as WRONG in the classic cartoon language. The figure's reaction: the classic strip pose of someone staring at something they can't believe. Clean lines, expressive, clear. No text.

panels.caption: There.
panels.prompt: Newspaper strip close-up panel. The eureka moment — a classic cartoon lightbulb moment but subdued. The face showing sudden recognition. Clean ink lines, strong expression, minimal background. Maybe a tiny spotlight effect. This is the classic newspaper strip economy: one face, one expression, total clarity. Black and white, spot highlight. No text.

panels.caption: Fix it. Ship it. Move on.
panels.prompt: Newspaper comic strip style. The figure typing fast — classic action lines, confident posture, the transformation from problem-having to problem-solving visible in the body language. Clean, clear, economical line work. A small smile. The screen now showing something that reads as CORRECT. Classic strip energy. No text.

panels.caption: 3:17 AM. Tests pass. The city doesn't know. Doesn't need to.
panels.prompt: Newspaper comic strip wide panel. The figure at the desk, leaning back, the quiet satisfaction of finished work. Simple city window view — a few lit windows, a moon. The scene drawn with the warmth and economy of a great Sunday strip. Clean lines, generous white space, honest emotion without sentimentality. One or two spot colors. The ending panel of a great strip. No text.
